Tomato Man
Porcupine mutant dangerous representative of the underworld with very strong armor.
A flying spike that appears from the hive. The spiny flies and seeks the protagonist to stab him to death.
Saws are the remains of mysterious and ancient technologies.
Cactus aggressive plant spreading thorns and interfering with the movement of the protagonist.
A dying volcano that can deliver no few problems to the protagonist.

Your standard X-11 Fighter. It has been used in nearly every confrontation since its development two decades ago, mainly because of its ease of manufacturing. It's not special in any way, but it's a quality piece of weaponry.

With its lightweight aluminum alloy frame and state-of-the-art ion propulsion system, this ship can reach greater speeds than other similar vehicles. The speed comes at a price though, since both the engine and frame aren't very durable.
Inspired by some of the first airplanes from Earth, this ship is able to manoeuvre with surprising accuracy. The downside is that it's hard to repair due to the intricate design.

In the year 2979, the United Nebula Coalition, with Lyle Ed Walsh as their lead engineer, designed and implemented this ship into their ranks. It features a superior firing mechanism, but this exact instance seems to be worse for wear.
This ship originates from a particularly nasty tribe on what was once called Mars. What they lack in the steering department, they certainly make up for in weapons design. This one hits hard.

Originally a ship used for transport of goods, but now partially adapted for use in battle. Its thick, sturdy hull provides high defence in a fight, but equally slows it down. It surely could be useful in the right hands.

This ship was actually not specifically designed for spacefaring, but for rugged terrain on a planets surface. It has seen a lot of use and has a few bumps and bruises, but its weapons seem to work, albeit probably not as well as they could.

Not much is known about this ship, or where it comes from. It's constructed of a strange material that seems to be repairing itself with extreme haste, but the weapon system looks inferior. It's also emitting a strange hum...
The beings who created this ship clearly had no intention of it being used in combat, but it is remarkably robust owing to the unique structure.

Created on Earth with long distance space travel in mind, this ship is fast as all hell, able to warp through space faster than the speed of light. Imagine what it takes to do a U-turn with this baby...
It's obvious that this beast was created for the sole purpose of destruction. Usually, this ship would enter battle with protection from several smaller allies since it has several weak spots.
A craft able to survive both the vacuum of space and the pressure of the ocean. The multi-functional propulsion doesn't output as much energy as a focused system, but it provides more control.
Brave English adventurer.

descriptions.type: Sentience: The Android's Tale Trading Card

descriptions.value: "I know that sometimes it seems like nobody here cares about our mission. The truth is, I do care."

descriptions.value: "Soulless you may be, but I believe you have the capacity to serve The Faith as well as any human."

descriptions.name: Jonah
descriptions.type: Sentience: The Android's Tale Trading Card

descriptions.value: "I think people underestimate this planet. There's more to it than meets the eye."

descriptions.name: Melis
descriptions.type: Sentience: The Android's Tale Trading Card

descriptions.value: "The Ten Robot Commandments. Imagine that. After all, the human version worked out so very well, didn't it?"

descriptions.name: Piers
descriptions.type: Sentience: The Android's Tale Trading Card

descriptions.value: "We love to anthropomorphise everything. They made you just human enough so that we'd make that mistake."

descriptions.name: Rocket
descriptions.type: Sentience: The Android's Tale Trading Card

descriptions.value: "Soon you will begin to witness the true wonders of this planet."

descriptions.name: Sylar
descriptions.type: Sentience: The Android's Tale Trading Card

descriptions.value: "I was meant for so much more than this worthless planet of dirt and dust."

descriptions.name: Walden
descriptions.type: Sentience: The Android's Tale Trading Card

descriptions.value: "Figures the company would send us an Origin class. Probably your last assignment before they send you to the scrap heap."
